verification_performed: Confirmed MLB_MODEL_INPUT_PATH_AUDIT_001 is ACTIVE on the board
Confirmed task brief and handoff are present in 05_INBOX_FROM_MANAGER
Confirmed the task remains paper-only / observation-mode work
Traced game-state source fields in game_state_service.py
Traced model feature assembly in winprob_inference.py
Traced market-state inputs and edge computation in market_state_stream.py
Traced recommendation output fields in recommendation_api.py
Traced bridge pass-through fields in sports_bot_v2/core/model_bridge.py

source_to_output_path: winprob_inference.infer_for_team(snapshot, tracked_team, pregame_prob) converts snapshot into model feature vector + feature_dict + data_quality
market_state_stream.compute_edge(...) combines model probability with market prices
sports_bot_v2/core/model_bridge.get_approved_intents(...) applies freshness/approval filters and passes selected fields into live intents

game_state_reaches_model.score: Yes, but compressed mainly through score_diff and related derived features
game_state_reaches_model.pitcher_state: Yes via home/away pitcher ids, pitch counts, bullpen flags in snapshot; model feature path clearly uses pitch counts and bullpen indicators
game_state_reaches_model.late_game_signal: Yes via late_game and walkoff_home flags in feature assembly

market_state_reaches_model_or_signal_lay.ask_yes_ask_no: Yes, but only in post-model market/edge layer, not in the inference feature vector shown in winprob_inference.py
market_state_reaches_model_or_signal_lay.spread: Yes, used in recommendation confidence through spread_quality
market_state_reaches_model_or_signal_lay.depth: Yes, emitted as thin_side_depth_usd and available in market state
market_state_reaches_model_or_signal_lay.market_price_awareness: Yes in edge computation and recommendation metadata, but not shown as a direct model input in winprob_inference.py
market_state_reaches_model_or_signal_lay.near_resolution_context: Indirect only, through market prices/costs after model inference, not as an explicit model feature in the audited path

confirmed_missing_or_compressed: Full score does not visibly survive as separate home_score and away_score in the recommendation output; it is compressed to score_diff in the audited path
Pitcher IDs are present in the raw snapshot layer but do not visibly survive into recommendation output or bridge output
Pitch counts and bullpen flags influence model features, but pitch-count detail itself does not visibly survive into recommendation output or bridge output
Base_state exists in the game-state snapshot and model feature assembly, but does not visibly survive into recommendation output or bridge output
Market price / ask state is not shown as a direct model input feature in winprob_inference.py; it enters after inference via edge computation and spread-quality adjustment
Near-resolution state is not represented as an explicit dedicated model feature in the audited inference path
missing_context_summary.interpretation: The current path is not missing basic game-state context. Score/inning/outs/base_state/pitcher-condition features do reach the model core. The bigger gaps are that several rich context fields are compressed or dropped before recommendation output, and market-price / near-resolution awareness appears to live mostly in the post-model edge/confidence layer rather than as explicit core inference features.

recommended_next_enabled_task: MLB_CONFIDENCE_CALIBRATION_AUDIT_001 and MLB_NEAR_RESOLUTION_SANITY_AUDIT_001 are now the next enabled tasks, because this audit established that basic game-state reaches the model while market-awareness and confidence meaning still need direct evaluation.
